message:
feat(extensions): migrate extension runtime from QuickJS/JS to native Rust

Replace the embedded QuickJS JavaScript extension runtime with a native
Rust runtime path, eliminating the JS marshalling boundary that was the
primary per-call overhead bottleneck (10-19x slower than in-process V8).

Core changes:
- Add NativeRustExtensionLoadSpec and NativeRustExtensionRuntimeHandle
  as first-class runtime variants alongside the existing Wasm path
- Deprecate JS/TS extension entrypoints with clear migration errors
  directing users to .native.json descriptors with runtime: "native-rust"
- Add ExtensionRuntime::NativeRust enum variant with serde rename
- Resolve .native.json index files before .ts/.js in extension discovery
- Reject JS runtime in validate_extension_manifest with actionable error

Agent/session layer:
- Rename PreWarmedJsRuntime → PreWarmedExtensionRuntime to reflect
  runtime-agnostic prewarm design
- Add start_native_extension_runtime() for inline native boot path
- Update enable_extensions_with_policy to classify specs by runtime type
  and route NativeRust specs through load_native_extensions()
- Handle pre-warmed runtime type mismatch (JS prewarm → native needed)
  with transparent fallback and structured tracing

Dispatcher abstraction:
- Introduce ExtensionDispatcherRuntime<C> trait to decouple dispatcher
  from concrete PiJsRuntime, enabling future native dispatch path
- All hostcall operations now go through self.js_runtime() accessor
- Constructor generics accept any R: ExtensionDispatcherRuntime<C>

Interactive/providers:
- Update js_runtime() → runtime() calls throughout interactive agent
- Wrap context payloads in Arc::new() for runtime command channel
- Update ExtensionStreamSimpleProvider to use ExtensionRuntimeHandle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
